title: Butterflies, Jellycat, and us
subtitle: A note after the first month together
paragraphs: Dear Ava,
I hope you enjoyed the butterfly Jellycat—your favorite animal—and the night we spent together.

This last month with you has truly been incredible, and getting to know you a little more every day has been such a pleasure. From your constant sass (in the best way), to your passion for all things cucumbers, to our nightly calls where you beg me to stay on the phone just five more minutes—aren’t I the worst—I find myself looking forward to every moment with you.

So let’s hypothetically clink our nostalgic red wine glasses to many more memories, special nights together, and the occasional saved sticker of your funny faces. Also, we are dating now so you can call back your brother and correct him (hm).

Love,
Lucas

title: Happy one month
subtitle: The jar, the memories, the real gifts
paragraphs: Dear Ava,

We’ve been together for one month now—30 days, 720 hours, 43,200 minutes, and 2,592,000 seconds by each other’s side. Even if that may seem small in the grand scheme of things, I wanted to show you just how much you truly mean to me.

Some of these memories are recent, others a little older, but every single one holds a special place in my heart. I made you this gift so you can revisit the moments we’ve shared—the big ones and the small ones. Whenever you miss me, feel sad, lonely, or overwhelmed, you can reach into the jar, pull one out, and remember that we’ve built something beautiful together.

This past month with you—and honestly, the past few months since we first started talking—has been one of the happiest times of my life. The first time my mom met you, she told me that when I walk into the room, your whole face lights up. There isn’t anything more I could ever ask for in a partner.

Here’s to many more months together—staying up too late on the phone, interrupting each other with a random “uhh,” trying the best food in the city, and creating countless more memories worth writing down.
Now… go open your real gifts.

Love,
Lucas

title: Valentine’s Day
subtitle: Honesty, ramen, and the ordinary magic
paragraphs: Dear Ava,
Happy Valentine’s Day!

I keep thinking about how, from the very beginning, you asked for honesty—real honesty, the kind that doesn’t hide behind charm or pretty words. So here it is: devoting myself to you has become one of the most grounding parts of my life. In a world that moves too fast and asks too much, you are the place where everything feels warm and steady.

Watching you in english class you always stood out as an anomaly. Catching you glancing at me, locking eyes, and blushing behind my chair was a daily occurrence. I even saw you whisper to Mac a couple of times saying “He’s so hot…” This doubled in history, when I would watch you struggle during our in-class writings, frantically stomping on your keyboard to make the time cut. For all of your genius, Pankow’s writings sometimes got the better of you.

I remember the day I made my first move, Sunday, November 23rd. I was sitting at Anton’s with Marcus and Allen waiting for our main courses––I ordered the lamb ragu, the chef’s recommendation of course. Allen started talking about my love life and if I had any new interest, and I made the decision to mention you and how your timid yet well-informed remarks struck me in English, and how you were easily always the best dressed in the room. Instead of going on a long tangent about how he “always picked up the hot chics” or “I was always too focused on medical school to care about women,” he asked for a picture. Dashing to my phone, I typed “avablav_” into the instagram search bar, and your personality appeared. He scrolled through your accounts and made small complimentary comments such as “she’s cute” and “I understand why you like her.” Then, he clicked on your photography account––I was starstruck. Your ability with a camera was a true talent and I wanted to continue seeing your photos on my feed. So, I made the bold decision to like a recent post and follow (still no follow back by the way). I thought my courageous action of clicking a small button through a digital screen would give you the indicator that I liked you.

From there, you added me on Snapchat and messaged me out of the blue after blackout––a half face with a subtle flirtatious smile. I was intrigued. I snapped back in an instant (17 minutes later) a picture of me eating ramen. Funny enough, since the moment we started talking we have been interlinked––you had also been eating ramen, with your infamous cookie monster pants on (unable to obtain) and a tank top.
I was hooked.
Ava, being a part of your life has never felt forced or complicated. It has felt steady. Intentional. Real.

I don’t just look forward to future Valentine’s Days. I look forward to the quiet Tuesdays at Peeky. The random Snapchats. The small glances across a room. The late-night dinners. The ordinary moments that somehow feel extraordinary when they’re with you.
Thank you for being my calm in the chaos. Thank you for your warmth, your mind, your heart, and the way you make life feel balanced.
Here’s to us and many more Valentine’s by your side.
Also, I haven’t given you your final Valentine’s Day gift yet. Here it is.

With Love and Affection,
Lucas

title: Happy 17th birthday
subtitle: Transparency, time, and how you stand apart
paragraphs: Dear Ava,
Happy 17th birthday!

On our first date, you told me that you value complete transparency in a relationship, so I want to honor that. When I turned 17, I had a panic attack. I felt like time was slipping through my receding hairline—that I was running out of my teenage years. And from one anxiety-driven person to another, I want you to remember this: take a breath, steady yourself, and know that the beauty of life comes from its limits. Time is what gives meaning to our choices, our memories, and the way we grow.

Ava, out of the thousands of faces I see every day, and the thousands I’ve met throughout my life, you stand apart. There is a fire in you that is inextinguishable—one I have never encountered before. From your smarts to your undying work ethic, to your endless “hms” and your 7:40 a.m. wake-ups without fail, I see just how capable you truly are.

So, Ava, make the most of your time. And every once in a while, look up from your Delta book and remember how special you are—how much you matter, how much you are loved by the ones around you, and how much of an impact you make.
Here’s to many more birthdays worth celebrating, and to more time spent alongside you.

Yours,
Lucas

title: my gift of moments
subtitle: under 3/1/26
paragraphs: I remember the first time we ever texted. The first snap I received from you was after Blackout. We began a streak of snapping back-and-forth, and after I sent you a snap of Leo on Tuesday, December 2nd, you swiped up and commented on how cute he is. I'm not fully sure if you called him Zawg, but there's a chance. Henceforth, we spent the next week staying up late at night talking for awhile––I still don't believe there were any flirtish feelings involved in the beginning.

I remember New Years day. We conversed the night before talking about the true reasons you were texting me (lol) and how your previous relations made you cautious. I also spoke about my past and how it left a mark on me as well. We both woke up at around the same times, with you being an hour ahead, and went on with our days, constantly updating one another. Then, 11:34pm hits my time, and I get a notification from you stating "Happy new years lucas!" (passive aggressive ahh). I sent you more snaps of my night and it was cute.

I remember when I first asked you out. We were both on winter break, and I secured a L'Artusi reservation for Friday, January 9 at 10:15pm––thinking now, that reservation was so late (we paid the bill at 12pm). I texted you over snap on the 28th of December asking for you to join me. You sent three emojis: ☺️☺️☺️.

I remember the first time we actually hung out. It was Saturday, January 3rd, and you were going out with Diana, Harper, and Daniel Shleifer. You were all visiting a piano bar later that night, and you invited me to see you prior. So, I walked from my house in the cold and waited outside your door. You rushed me in, wanting me to introduce myself to your Mom, Emily. In a nervous fashion, I shaked her hand, said "Wow, what a beautiful house," and spoke to her for a couple of minutes. We rushed out the door and made a lap around the city, walking all the way to 59th and Lexington.

I remember our first date. We planned on L'Artusi, 10:15pm, so I naturally showed up at 10 to confirm our reservation and put my card down. You, as you always do (in a nice way), showed up at 10:30, wearing your wonderful black and white jacket (the bougie one), long hoop earrings, navy blue jeans, and a sleeveless black top. We had a wonderful conversation––stemming from topics of sophistication to relationship topics––ate delicious food, and met Julia and Daniel Silverman. When I finally paid, I left that little note at the topic that you will eventually see on Sunday, March 1st. We walked around the West Village, talking and laughing, stopping at Milk Bar to get a crack pie. We continued our walk until we stumbled up on Zach and got a ride home. I have more details about the night, but fitting them all into one paragraph would be a challenge. Just ask, and I'll tell you.

I remember Tuesday, January 13th when you weren't feeling well and missed school. I was concerned about your wellbeing for the greater part of the day, and decided to see you later that night and bring Leo along with me. We stayed in your dining room, talking for close to two hours, before you Mom texted you and asked me politely to leave. The way you were flapping Leo's ears made my heart melt.

I remember our second date. After our first date on Friday, I met with Allen and his lovely wife Ellen for dinner that following Sunday. You came up in conversation and I asked for a reservation suggestion; Allen took my phone and searched for Spes. He showed me pictures of the food, atmosphere, space, etc, and I settled on it: Friday, January 16th at 9pm. I arrived early, you arrived late (hehehe), and we sat down for an incredible dinner. We ordered the octopus and the pomodoro, but the highlight of the night were the beverages. Your outfit was as dazzling as ever and I showed up in a suit 😣.

I remember our time after our second date. We chose the nostalgic wine as our preferred drink and three glasses later we were in the car with one of your drivers––he seemed middle eastern idrk––heading back to my house. You were lying on me in the car filming tiktoks and snapping Diana while I was just excited to get back to my house. We walked into the house meeting my brother and his friend Harrison, quickly scurrying to my room. An entire night unfolded and we finally kissed, cuddled, and had a lot of fun. You stayed a little later than your curfew and you ended up getting home near 1am (oops).

I remember seeing you in the morning after our second date. I woke up with snow slowly covering New York, texting you to see how you were doing, primarily worried that you had sustained a hangover. I picked up some matchas from Blank Street, and walked them over to your house, slowly sipping yours (oops) on the way there. We chilled in your bed, conversing on the night before and our plans later on in the day, and your friends arrived––Diana and Harper. You took some adderall in hopes of locking in and had a scare when I mentioned something that made you feel that we were not going to commit––look at us now.

I remember seeing you for our cute little two man with Diana and Ethan. You guys were out to Mac's party living it up while I was on the phone, convincing him to come over. We settled on a time, 11:15pm, and he showed up a while earlier. We hung out, discussed his breakup with Remi, and you two decided to show up around 11:30, per usual (hm). We were chilling on the couch, having a dry conversation, and we made our way to my room to let Diana make her move. You and I cuddled, kissed for a bit, and made our way back to the living room an hour later, so that both of you wouldn't be late to Greenwich. We kissed goodbye and you spent the remainder of your night fighting off the demons.

I remember our third date. You insisted on the same place, Spes, being the creature of habit that you are––apparently inherited from your parents because Val has the same thing. We spent our times leading up to the date differently: you went to the dentist with Diana and her sister and I hung out with Ryan, worked out, steamed, and ate a bit. This time, I was late to the date and you were early––my Mom slowed me down because she got in a fight with my Dad. I remember explaining my situation to you and you stressed that I shouldn't confront my Dad––I followed your advice and never did. We had such an incredible time at dinner and afterward…

I remember our time after our third date. We walked out of the restaurant a bit tipsy, looking for Zach in the cold weather. You were wearing a black lined top, with short sleeves, coupled with some black jeans, while I was wearing my iconic blue sweater and a puffed fur jacket. We navigated the New York streets looking for Zach––his location revealed that he was situated an avenue away. Passing through the block, we encountered a hectic fire scene. I heroically ran into the building, saving a baby and a dog, but you didn't see that part I guess. We found Zach, and began driving to my house, talking about the various cars you do not like to be driven in, emphasis on the old maybachs. When we got to my apartment, we found our ways to my room and began hooking up, finally eyp for the first time––but you didn't finish 🙁. You left really late, closer to 1:30am, and when you got home we called as you ate cucumber salad.

I remember being invited over for dinner with your mother, sister, and her boyfriend, Connor. It was 5pm, and I was still at Ryan's house playing 2k as you urgently texted me stating "My mom invited you for dinner tonight at 7:30pm". I quickly rushed home, showered, changed, and walked over to your house with Godiva chocolates in hand. The evening was fun and relaxing––we spoke about Connor's fast food company, your Mom potentially buying a hotel of sorts, and Stagify. Then, we made our way upstairs for some Rummikub. Laila dominated, as usual, as I slowly learned the ins-and-outs of the game. We finished with a quick debrief of the night, alone, and I made my way home.

I remember the day I knew you wanted to date. It was Friday, January 30th, and Diana and you were out at someone's birthday party––you wore a fabulous dress with holes of sorts cut in the middle of the dress. I asked you to come over after the party had concluded, and you agreed, on the basis I socially distance. We spoke for a bit because I was falling asleep from my NyQuil, and Diana called me later that night telling me that this was the time. Diana the GOAT.

I remember the day we began dating––February 1st. The day prior, I ran around the city picking up cooking supplies for the evening. My first stop was Citerella, buying some pasta, sauce, garlic, etc. I later walked to Lobel's, picking up a New York strip. I arrived at 7:30pm to find that the house was desolate. You opened the door in nice clothing––I don't remember exactly––and I made my way downstairs to begin cooking. While I was cooking I acknowledged that I viewed you as my girlfriend, and you stated the feeling was mutual––but we say we started dating on the 1st because not every date has a 31st! I first finished the pasta, then the steak, cutting it into two halves––one to my liking, rare, and one to yours, medium well. We quickly ate, cleaned, and made our way upstairs to cuddle, talk, and rot. I handed you smucas, you love him, and I left closer to 1:30am.

I remember the day I dropped off my soil pot from synagogue. When I got there at 6:30pm, we were each handed a flower pot and some colored pencils. Coincidentally, I was handed green (sagish color) and purple. I colored my heart out on the soil pot, and thought it would be a cute daily reminder of the growth of our relationship. Later that night I walked to your house, dropped it off with your security, and walked home.

I remember the day you came to my hockey game at Riverbank against Fieldston. We showed up ready to lose, since our previous game against this school was an embarrassment: 12-1 final score. I urged you not to come in fear of us losing once again, but you stuck with your word and came. We played our hearts out, somehow winning 4-2. I watched you talk to my Mom and Diana during the game, occasionally smiling in my direction now-and-again. After the game, we made our way to Nightly's. We ordered a salad to share as well as a steak, truffle mac-and-cheese on the side. We had an amazing time and my parents loved you. You were so squish that night.

I remember our fun Friday party together. I had a hockey game earlier in the night so I had to rush home, change, and make it to the pre. I showed up early to the party, making my way to the DJ booth to spend time with Spencer. You arrived later on in the night wearing a beautiful outfit: black ski jumpsuit covered in large white stars coupled with a skirt, bougie glasses, and a shapka (russian hat). We danced the entire time, making our way back-and-forth to the DJ booth, queuing a new song, dancing, and then rejoining our friends––of course we got cute pictures too. Then, as soon as the party began, it ended, and we made our way to Zach.

I remember our time after the party. We both rushed into the car with Diana, Mac, and Max because we were freezing. On our way back, you were lying on me while we all spoke about the party and our night out. We first stopped at your house, but you instructed Zach to drop Mac off first––maybe it was you being nice, maybe it was you wanting to spend more time with me…On our way to Mac's, we stopped to get some food; I picked up some Gnocchi while you got Tacos (ewwww). We dropped Mac off, made our way back to yours, and went our separate ways––Diana and Max, Ava and Lucas. We cuddled for the greater part of an hour, scrolling on reels and squeezing cheeks every now-and-then––thank god your Mom didn't come down. I left around 3am with Max, discussing our nights and talking about Diana (hehehe).

I remember the day I first met Julia with you. You guys were at some party, Julia's Model Congress friends. They were rude and obnoxious, as all people are (pessimist mindset 💪), and I remember one of them texted you, begging to come to your bday party. I ran downstairs and jumped in the car, awkwardly meeting Julia for the first time as Zach turned the corner, made his way up 3rd avenue, and parked next to El Taco, for the second night in a row. We made tiktoks inside, finished our food, and swiftly left to spend a couple minutes at mine before you had to leave for Greenwich. I still remember how badly Julia had to pee, and how she was weirdly recording us just chilling––opp behavior if you ask me.

I remember stopping by briefly when I was on my run to say hi to you and Laila. I was on my not-so-daily run to your house-and-back––remember it's perfectly a mile away, i'm not a creep––and I asked for you to come out and say hi. You invited me in and we sat down with Laila in your Mom's office while you two were starting a series. We briefly spoke about Yale etc, and we left.

I remember you giving me a ride home on your way to the doctor. We both finished school at the same time, and I saw you exiting, so I asked you for a ride. You were extremely sick at the moment and needed medical attention ASAP, so obviously you had a doctor's appointment the same day––you could not be sick during your trip. We hopped in the car while waiting at Diana's house––the other Diana took forever––and we jumped inside with Elvis. We talked about our day, spoke about Diana and how I want to set her up with Ryan, and we arrived, circling the block a couple of times––I remember mistaking my pediatrician for yours. You later sent me a photo of you getting your temperature taken, how smush.

I remember Valentine's Day. You showed up to my hockey game, we lost, and I had to rush home to change for dinner. Diana gracefully gave my mother and I a ride home while you and Eva were also in the car––my Mom tried to spark some weird convo with Eva and I was super embarrassed. When I later arrived at your house I sat on your chair facing your desk while you were putting on makeup fresh out of the shower. We exchanged gifts and I was enamored by the lovely scarfs, hats, socks, robes, and Undercover Quinoa that besieged my eyes––your letter was also beautiful and it's now housed underneath my night table. You also wore quite a wonderful outfit: black jeans, sleeveless black top, a red coat, plus an additional scarf, the perfect cherry on top. We made our way to Tsumo for some omakase, and we both ordered the special, conversing and laughing while we were each handed our respective pieces––your uneaten caviar made my night. After dinner, we made our way back to yours and spent close to an hour cuddling and talking, both excited for our trips away over the weekend, but sad to leave each other. Checking our phones, it was 9:45pm, and I got scared for my safety (Mom would kill me), so I ran out of your house, clarifying to your Mom that we were "only talking on the coach and lost track of time". What an adorable evening.

I remember posting for Valentine's Day. It was the first hard launch, except for the tiktok, and it was super cute. We chose a black-and-white picture of us after our third date at Spes, and I received countless likes on the post––I also remember you stating that it was your most viewed story ever (hehe). Val later swiped up and asked for dinner plans that never happened––I wonder how that would have gone.

I remember staying up with you on your final night on the boat. I was on my way to the airport from Lake Tahoe, and we didn't leave the chat for the next two hours. I brought up how I've always noticed a glimmer in your eyes when you look at home and how it's unmistakable. We also spoke about limerence and how although it may seem beautiful, it is only an illusion, and it is unhealthy to the user––obsession isn't love.

I remember your birthday party at Nobu. I spent the greater part of the day swimming and working out with Ryan in order to look appealing for the evening. Rushing home afterward, I changed into a button down, jeans, and quarter zip, getting screamed at by my Dad in the process for potentially being late––I also made sure to bring my flowers as well. I arrived at your house at 6:28pm, meeting Laila at the door, asking her about her weekend and if Connor will be joining us. I went upstairs to greet you and Julia, just in time for the birthday tiktok and pictures, taking sexy ones by the mirror in your bathroom––Val and Laila later criticized the story 👀. We later walked downstairs, rushing to the car, and I met your Dad for the first time; looking back, I was extremely nervous in the moment, but in reality he has a cool, calm presence to him. We went to the restaurant, ate incredible food, took lovely pictures, and had interesting conversations––emphasis on train usage in China during the New Years. I came back with you, hung out in your room for a bit, and went home. Thank you for inviting me ♥️.

I remember coming with you to Greenwich. I was on my way back from Model Congress, leaving a day early in fears of getting stuck in Boston amidst the blizzard. On my way back, while conversing with you, I brought up the idea of visiting Greenwich, and spending time with you and your loved ones. Val, thankfully, vouched for my presence, and I had to change mid bus ride to look presentable. When I arrived at your house in the city, I was greeted by your British friends, Arianna and _____ (I forgot shhh), and I learned about their chaotic night staying with you––Arianna hooked up with Ethan ____ (I also forgot) and she kinda pussied out. She was also disturbed the entire time by your childish ways; apparently you were blasting loud sex music while they were in the act. After dropping them off, and debriefing with Zach about crypto, we arrived at your house in Greenwich, and I greeted your entire family. Interrogation time with Val was fun, and now I see where you get your humor from. We spent the rest of our evening talking, drinking tea, cuddling, and experimenting with Cetaphil. When we woke up we got straight to work completing our homework for the weekend––amisdt our stress, we received the notification that school would be cancelled. Later on, we ate lunch where Val got into a disagreement with your Sister and Mother about their views about women in the common era, went maple tapping, and eventually left. I had an incredible time.

I remember all the nights we stayed up late on the phone, unraveling the ins and outs of who we are — exploring our individual identities piece by piece. In those quiet hours, I always learn so much about you. Your beauty stretches far beyond appearances; it lives in the way you think, the way you feel, the way you see the world. The vastness of your mind reminds me of the ocean—deep, powerful, and endless in its depth.

I remember you adding me on snapchat and our first snaps together. A quick ad notification popped up saying, avablav wants to be your friend. I was a little taken back because I never knew this girl in my class had any thoughts about me. You snapped me first after blackout––a half face with a subtle flirtatious smile. I snapped back in an instant (17 minutes later) a picture of me eating ramen. Fate has it we were both eating ramen. Guess they wanted us together or something…

I remember coming to your real birthday party (27th of February). I walked into the mid-section of the door (area between the outside and the real restaurant), adjusting my pant leg, and I see you bend down so you can see me through the door, waiving with an intense smile on your face. Opening the real door, I give you a massive hug, kiss you on the cheek, and compliment you on your outfit––wow, your dress that night was stunning, skews of skews of tulip purple lined across your body, with shades of red and black surrounding them. I also remember your beauty that night––your makeup highlighted your eyes, the center point of your appearance. Shaded black, with hints of red and, once again, purple dazzled your countenance. We ate, we sang, we partied, and we took many, many photos. It was a wonderful occasion and I had the best evening. Afterward, we headed to your friends gym, worked out a bit, went back to yours, and lied in bed talking and sleeping, for the most part, until I left at 3am. I love these occasions with you.

title: Columbia thank-you
subtitle: What your belief has meant
paragraphs: Dear Ava,

I am writing this letter amidst the chaos of crafting ones for your mother, father, and Ms. Kim, but I felt it was important to tell you just how much your presence has meant to me—far more than I could ever fully express alongside the others.

Ava, from the very first time we spoke, you had an innate ability to make me feel hopeful. The way you would casually say that “it will all work out,” or that I would “do good nonetheless,” never felt like empty reassurance—it felt real. Those small comments became something much bigger, forming a foundation of support that carried me through even my most uncertain days.

But above all else, I must thank you for always believing in me. You said I would get into Columbia when I wasn’t sure myself, and you took a chance on me when it mattered most. That belief meant more than I can fully put into words—it gave me confidence when I needed it most.

I still remember the first night you told me about the opportunity your dad had offered—the night of our first school party together, February 7th. While everything else was going on around us, we spent most of the night just lying there, talking about the future, about what could be, and about everything that felt so uncertain at the time. It’s strange how a moment that seemed so simple then has stayed with me so clearly. I think it’s because that was one of the first times I truly felt that someone saw something in me before I could see it in myself—and that someone was you.

Ava, you have changed my life––I cannot stress this enough. No matter what happens in our future, I will always carry immense gratitude for you, your heart, and your selfless nature; I couldn’t ask for anything more in a partner. I look forward to sharing the city for at least another year, continuing our passion for trying new restaurants, the occasional art performance, and nights tucked away in your bed. Ava, I love you, and yes, I mean it. Ur butt hehehehe.

With Immense Love,
Lucas

title: Happy two months
subtitle: Spes, dreams, and not letting go
paragraphs: Dear Ava,
Happy two months!

Although our time together feels minuscule in comparison to the years we have lived apart from one another, I want to stress how much your presence—the silky, warm body I lie beside on the weekend, the funny mouth that whispers “ur butt” on our walks around 64th, and the curious soul who constantly asks to tell her something interesting—has changed the very chemistry of my brain. Ava, words cannot begin to describe how much of an impact you have made on my life.

In light of my previous gift, I want to share my favorite memory with you, to stifle your unusual amnesia. It was our third date, January 24th, and I spent the majority of my afternoon exercising with Ryan and steaming with him afterward. I remember discussing my excitement for our third date together, and how we morphed into creatures of habit, rushing back to the same spot as before, Spes. Ryan discussed his new fling with a girl in the grade below, Cassie, and how he was most likely going to see her later on in the night.

When we finished, I rushed upstairs to his room, throwing on my clothes, and making my way home, to make sure I wouldn’t be late to dinner. When I got home, my parents got into a major argument, with some hurt feelings back and forth, and I waited on my Mom to conclude the fight. On our way to the restaurant, she confessed to me how my father, over the years, consistently lost his ambition for their marriage––how his lack of action made her feel unwanted––I tear ran down my eye when I noticed her bug eyes curdle with teardrops. We later pulled over on the highway so I could drive while she gathered herself in the backseat; I became a part-time chauffeur that day (hehehe).

After finally arriving at the restaurant at 8:12pm (Ava activities), I rushed inside, greeting you at the counter––I remember being upset that they sat us on a corner of the high tops. We moved, sat down comfortably, and ordered our favorites: the grilled octopus tentacles (Spain) with potatoes and parsley, as well as the spaghettoni with three tomato varieties and basil.

When I first lifted my head from the menu and finally got a proper look at you, your beauty caught me completely off guard—the soft waves of your dark hair framed your face so effortlessly, like they’d been arranged by instinct rather than effort, and your eyes carried this quiet warmth that made everything around us feel a little less important. The way your black outfit hugged your silhouette was simple but striking, elegant without trying, and when you smiled—just slightly, like you weren’t even aware of it—it felt genuine in a way that’s rare, the kind of smile that doesn’t ask for attention but holds it anyway. There was something about the calm confidence in how you stood, the ease in your posture, that made the moment linger longer than it should have. That night, I knew that our time together would mean more than a simple relationship––it felt like the beginning of something that would quietly shape me, the kind of connection that settles in your chest before you even have the words for it. Standing there beside you, with your arm resting so naturally and that soft, knowing look in your eyes, it wasn’t just attraction—it was a sense of ease, like I’d somehow arrived somewhere I didn’t know I’d been searching for.
And somehow, even after that night ended, you never really left my mind—you lingered in ways I couldn’t explain, finding your way into the quiet spaces of my thoughts, and even into my dreams.

I believe God places the most significant people in our dreams as a gentle recurring reminder of who we must never let go—you’ve appeared on ten different occasions in the last month. Ava, no matter what the future holds for us, I don’t want to lose you throughout the process.

To many more months alongside each other, celebrating the many small milestones, grand in meaning—the quiet routines that become ours without noticing, the laughter that lingers longer than it should, and the fleeting moments that, somehow, end up mattering the most.

With Love & Affection,
Lucas

title: Three months together
subtitle: Strings, distance, and what “I love you” meant
paragraphs: Dear Ava,
Wow, three months together already––this time together has really flown by.

It’s funny how some people you didn’t even talk to months ago have now become your whole life––it’s as if there are these invisible strings guiding individuals to one another throughout their lives. Who knew that our strings, the same ones alongside each other during the study of films in Daddy’s class, or the history of the overdone Amendments would eventually tangle, and lead to the beauty we now have.
I know that throughout the past months you might be sick of our constant letters and/or gifts, but I want to stress to you how much you truly mean to me.

Ava, when I first mentioned that I loved you––Saturday, April 11––it wasn’t out of a place of insecurity, or a need to hold onto something too tightly. It wasn’t because I was bored with my life or just wanted to lie to you at the moment, even though you claim I’m a pathological liar (hehehe). My proclamation of love came from a place of clarity, clarity caused by distance.

As you know, I’ve always been the kind of person who looks at life through the lens of optimism—the type who believes the glass is never half empty, but instead half full, no matter the circumstances. I tend to search for the good in situations, even when things feel uncertain or difficult, and I try to hold onto the belief that there is always something meaningful to take away from every experience.

Although the times we spend apart from one another may hurt, and sometimes even lead to disagreements, it has taught me just how much I value what we have. During our three weeks apart over spring break, while you were making your way through Shanghai, Hong Kong, and Beijing, and I was wandering through the golden temples of Japan, I realized something I couldn’t ignore: even in the middle of beautiful places and new experiences, there was an ache that followed me. A quiet, constant feeling that something was missing.
And that something was you.

It wasn’t just that I missed talking to you 1-on-1 or seeing you—it was deeper than that. I felt the absence of your presence in the smallest moments––the nighttime walks, the occasional flinch when you lift your hand, and the winced countenance that appears when I lean in for a kiss. That feeling made it clear to me that being apart doesn’t just create distance—it reveals how much my emotions are tied to you, how much brighter everything feels when you’re there with me.
That’s what I meant when I said I loved you. It wasn’t impulsive—it was something I came to understand, fully and honestly, in those moments when I felt your absence the most.

Yes, together we will face challenges, but we will surely succeed. Being apart has made me appreciate you more, not less. It reminded me that love isn’t just about the easy, happy times, but also about patience, understanding, and growing together through the difficult ones. And no matter the distance or the disagreements, I always find my way back to you.

I know the sudden shift from high school to college may be strenuous at times, but it proves to be another mountain we must climb. Another adventure that we will partake in. Another walk around the block, shivering in the cold while discussing the drama in our lives. Luckily, this shift in location will only be a 20 minute car drive away. Instead of our mountain posing itself as Everest, we will experience the beauty, sandy pits of an ant hill. We will push through, day-by-day, living in the moment, with our love growing to new heights.
Ava, I love you and can’t wait to see what’s ahead of us. To many more monthly celebrations alongside one another, long nights sneaking wine underneath the table, and aftercare rituals.

With Immense Love,
Lucas

title: Happy four months
subtitle: Diamonds, pressure, and trust
paragraphs: Dear Ava,
Happy four months!

I am writing this on Monday, May 25th, after our night of complications in the Hamptons. Although we have moments of stress, anger, resentment, and mixed emotions, I want to emphasize that this is what love is actually about. If we didn't have these moments—if we were just some seemingly perfect, frictionless couple—I would doubt that we even cared deeply enough to be hurt by one another. So, please don't view our moments of pressure as distasteful or hazardous. Take a more optimistic approach, as I try to do, and view them as the safe, intense pressures that turn plain carbon-12 (the most abundant carbon isotope) into diamonds—like the heart-shaped one lying against your neck right now.

Moving aside from our flaws—the same beautiful imperfections that turn scars into unique skin, or broken bones into stronger ones—I want to give you another specific moment where time froze alongside you, and I truly felt that what we had was entirely special.

It was March 21st, later that evening. You spent the first half of the day directing in Brooklyn for your friend, and I spent my day preparing for my presentations and getting my work sorted before the break. We agreed on meeting that night for a goodbye before we departed on our individual trips across the world.

You arrived at my place at 7:00 PM with no real plan in mind, other than to relax and just enjoy each other's company. We hopped into bed, hugging and cuddling beneath the blanket, until we reached the topic of virginity, and when we would finally cross that line together. In all honesty, even before we spoke, I had a feeling we were approaching that beautiful turning point. But because we had taken our time beforehand, jumping into that territory took me by surprise in the absolute best way possible. I knew in my heart I was ready, and looking at you, I knew you were too.

My hand rushed to my nightside table, pulling open the drawer to grab the condoms I had stowed away—and, trying to be prepared, the Astroglide to make sure it was as gentle and easy on your body as possible. The heat in the room was almost thick enough to touch. I knelt over you, the cool slide of the lubricant a stark contrast to the sudden, spiking warmth between us.

You watched me from the pillow, your chest rising and falling in a quick, shallow rhythm. There was a raw, beautiful tension in the way you looked up at me—that distinct, thrilling edge of someone standing on the brink of something entirely new. The dim light caught the line of your collarbone, casting long shadows across your bare skin as you took a slow, steadying breath.

I leaned down, shifting my weight until I was hovering just inches above you, letting the anticipation stretch out until it was almost unbearable. Your hands reached up, fingers tracing a hesitant, burning path against my hips. I closed the remaining distance, and the quiet friction of skin on skin felt like the beginning of an entirely new language between us.
My tip pressed smoothly against you, parting a way for its entry. You were entirely mine, completely open to me, and you trusted me implicitly.

I'll stop the story before the windows begin to steam up, but looking back at that night reminds me of the flawless foundation we've built. That level of absolute vulnerability and complete openness is incredibly rare, Ava, and I don't take it for granted for a single second. I trust you entirely, and knowing that I have that same trust from you means everything to me.

Every relationship has its storms, but I wouldn't want to navigate them with anyone else. I love you—flaws, pressures, diamonds, and all. I can't wait for all the moments, the trips, and the quiet nights still ahead of us. Happy four months, my love. Here's to many more.

With Trust,
Lucas

title: Happy five months
subtitle: Whoop data, sleep scores, and missing you
paragraphs: Dear Ava,
Happy Five Months!

Throughout these last couple of months alongside you, I have learned one valuable lesson: Do not wear a Whoop around Ava Blavatnik. One glance at the shiny black alien technology on my wrist and my Queen immediately exclaims, "that damn Whoop." Although your hatred for fitness-metric technology is well documented, its insight has shown me something surprising.

Since you left on June 5th, my average night of sleep has been horrible. More precisely, my sleep score has averaged around 70% instead of the 88% it normally hovers around, with a standard deviation of 4%. Breaking this down mathematically, my recent sleep scores have produced a z-score of roughly -4.5, meaning they are 4.5 standard deviations below my normal average. Statistically speaking, that is extraordinarily rare. Assuming a normal distribution, sleep scores this low would occur only about 3-4 times in a million observations.

"So what does this mean?" you might be asking. Well, there are only two possible explanations: Either I have suddenly developed a previously undiscovered sleep disorder that has baffled modern medicine, or I simply sleep much worse when you're not here. Given that one of those explanations is significantly more flattering to you, I think we should go with the second.
My Whoop has provided definitive scientific proof that I miss you, and your presence. New York is not the same without you, and I only feel at ease when we are blocks apart.
Five months is a bit of an odd milestone, so I felt that there would be much more to write for the sixth month, that's why I made this letter a bit of fun.
I love you and cannot wait to spend more time together.

With Yearn,
Lucas
